revision.text:
[[ja:上溝天神塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun | image = [[File:Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Rear circular mound and stone chamber entrance
(Upper left: Tenjin Shrine building) | Other name = | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|qid=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun Group|1=Iida Kofun group}} (Matsuo Unit Group)
Agemizo Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|qid=Q841129|lt=Iida City|1=Iida, Nagano}}, Matsuo Agemizo, Nagano Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|35.50219444444444|N|137.84522222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-20 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length: 41.5m
Height: 7.9m (rear circular mound) | burial = No-sleeve {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Tombs = | built = Mid-6th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|qid=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun Group|1=Iida Kofun group}} | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = Japan Nagano | type = 前方後円墳-西 | map_dot_label = Agemizo
Tenjinzuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun''' (上溝天神塚古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tomb located in Matsuo Agemizo, {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|qid=Q841129|lt=Iida City|1=Iida, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|qid=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun Group|1=Iida Kofun group}} (specifically the Matsuo Unit Group) and is designated as a National Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tenjin Shrine building (atop the rear circular mound)|qq=}}]] The tomb is situated at the southern end of a tongue-shaped terrace on the southern bank of the {{ill|Matsukawa River|ja|松川 (飯田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsukawa River|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsukawa River|1=Matsukawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11530260}} (a tributary of the Tenryu River) at an elevation of 423 meters. The mound is currently occupied by a Tenjin Shrine and a community hall, and the site has undergone significant alterations and leveling over time. In 1991, the collapse of the stone chamber entrance prompted cleaning and detailed measurement surveys. The tomb is keyhole-shaped, with the front section oriented to the west. The burial facility is a no-sleeve horizontal stone chamber located in the rear circular mound, opening southward. The chamber measures 10.8 meters in total length and features large natural stones as its primary construction material. Numerous grave goods, including mirrors, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, horse tack, belt fittings, golden rings, beads, haji ware, and decorated sue ware, were recovered from the chamber. The tomb is dated to the mid-6th century[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/agemizotenjinduka.html Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun] (Iida City official website). (or early 6th century {{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}), with evidence suggesting reuse or rituals extending into the late 7th to late 8th centuries{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}. The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 2016 and is now open to the public following the restoration of the stone chamber. == Historical Timeline == * {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: A Tenjin Shrine was constructed atop the mound{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}. * Early {{ill|20th century|en|20th century|qid=Q6927|lt=20th century|1=20th century}}: A community hall was built{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}. * 1983: Excavation of the front section of the mound and surrounding moat prior to hall reconstruction{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}. * 1983–1984: Detailed measurement of the stone chamber by [[Shiraishi Taichiro]] and others (reported in 1988). * 1991: Stone chamber cleaning and measurement survey following entrance collapse. * 2016: Designation as a National Historic Site (as part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|qid=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun Group|1=Iida Kofun group}}). == Mound area == The dimensions of the mound are as follows{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}: * Total length: 41.5 meters * Rear circular mound: ** Diameter: 22.5 meters ** Height: 7.9 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Width: 31.0 meters ** Height: 2.7 meters == burial == [[File:Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] [[File:Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded diagram of the stone chamber|qq=}}]] The burial facility is a no-sleeve horizontal stone chamber in the rear circular mound, opening southward. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}: * Total length: 10.8 meters * Burial chamber: ** Length: 7.7 meters ** Width: 2.4–2.7 meters ** Height: 2.6–3.0 meters * Entrance corridor: ** Length: 3.0 meters ** Width: 1.8 meters ** Height: 2.2 meters Large natural stones were used as materials for the chamber. The burial chamber and corridor are separated by a lowered ceiling{{Sfn|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}. The current sleeve stones and floor are reconstructions based on excavationsInterpretive board at the historic site.. Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Overview of the stone chamber Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber (toward the back wall) Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber (toward the entrance) Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Artifacts == [[File:上溝天神塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Artifacts on display|qq=}}{{small|Exhibited at the Iida City Archaeological Museum.|qq=}}]] ; Artifacts of uncertain origin: * Mirror (1) * Iron arrowheads (10) * Possible sword pommel ; Artifacts from 1983 excavation: * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}: Bowls and high-footed bowls * Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} ; Artifacts from 1991 cleaning survey: * Sword fittings: ** Silver pommel with a rectangular type (1) ** Handguard (2) ** End cap (3) ** Possible scabbard tip (1) * Numerous iron arrowheads * Horse tack: ** Decorative bridle with large circular plaques (2) ** Reins (1) ** Numerous fastening fittings ** Ring-shaped fittings ** Hinge fittings (10 or more) ** Decorative ornaments (2) * Belt pendant ornaments (1 or more) * Golden rings (6) * Beads: ** Silver hollow beads (6) ** Silver spindle-shaped beads (2) ** Gold-plated spindle-shaped beads (4) ** Large glass beads (8) ** Small glass beads (65) ** Lacquered beads (10) ** Tubular beads (2) ** Spindle-shaped beads (11) * Decorated {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (1) * {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}: Bowls with incised designs (1) == Related Facilities == * Iida City Archaeological Museum (Iida City, Kamigō Beppu) — Stores and exhibits artifacts from Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in this article:|qq=}} * Interpretive board by the Iida City Board of Education (installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Reference Materials|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida|2007|qq=}}|qq=}} — Link from the Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun|qq=}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/agemizotenjinduka.html Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun] — Official website of Iida City {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|上溝天神塚古墳|version=99996043|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上溝天神塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Agemizo Tenjinzuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552778}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:飯田古墳群]] [[Category:飯田市の歴史]]
